result.summary: Google App Engine exposes the App Engine Admin HTTP API at `https://appengine.googleapis.com/` plus the `gcloud app` CLI; both use Google Cloud OAuth-based authentication.
result.description: Google App Engine is Google Cloud’s managed application hosting platform for deploying and running web applications and services. It provides standard and flexible environments and related management features such as versions, services, domain mappings, and firewall rules.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token
gcp_oauth.setup: Use any standard Google Cloud auth flow that yields an access token for your project identity. The easiest paths in the docs are to sign into the CLI with [`gcloud auth login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/authenticate) for user credentials, or set up Application Default Credentials / a service account for REST and client-library use from [Authenticate for using REST](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/rest). The App Engine Admin API requires one of the OAuth scopes `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/appengine.admin`, `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`, or for some read-only methods `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform.read-only`.
